seoTitle: Science after 10th — courses, exams & careers | SkillKite
seoDescription: After 10th Science — streams, PCM/PCB, polytechnic, paramedical & more

guide.heading: Your options after 10th — SkillKite
guide.greeting: Hi there! You told us you are interested in Science with Maths and want to both study and earn. The most relevant options for you are listed first — read through all of them before deciding.

sections.title: Study options
sections.intro: Most relevant option is listed first — but read all options before deciding.

options.name: 12th Science with Maths (PCM)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in 11th and 12th. This is the most direct path if you enjoy numbers, logic, and problem-solving.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy Maths and want to enter engineering, technology, or applied sciences.
options.leadsTo: B.Tech / B.E. (engineering), B.Sc. Maths/Physics, Architecture (B.Arch), NDA (defence), merchant navy, and many more.
options.keyExams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced (for IITs), state-level CETs (MHT-CET, KCET, etc.), NDA exam
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3–4 years for a degree

options.name: 12th Science with Maths + Biology (PCMB)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, Maths, AND Biology together. It keeps all doors open but the workload is significantly heavier.
options.whoFor: Students who are genuinely strong in both Maths and Biology and have not yet decided between engineering and medicine.
options.leadsTo: All engineering options (via JEE) AND medical/paramedical options (via NEET). Maximum flexibility.
options.keyExams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, NEET, state CETs
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th — workload is heavier than PCM or PCB alone

options.name: Polytechnic Diploma (3 years, direct after 10th)
options.whatIsIt: A 3-year technical diploma in branches like Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Computer Science, or Electronics. You can start earning faster and even join degree engineering later via lateral entry.
options.whoFor: Students who want hands-on technical skills quickly, or who want to earn while continuing education later.
options.leadsTo: Junior Engineer roles, government jobs (SSC JE), private industry jobs, or lateral entry into 2nd year B.Tech/B.E.
options.keyExams: State polytechnic entrance exams (e.g., UP JEECUP, Delhi CET, AP POLYCET) — admission criteria vary by state, some are merit-based

options.name: 12th Science with Biology (PCB)
options.whatIsIt: You study Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. This is the standard path for students aiming for medical and health sciences.
options.whoFor: Students who are more interested in biology and healthcare than in Maths-heavy engineering.
options.leadsTo: MBBS, BDS, BAMS, B.Pharm, B.Sc. Nursing, Physiotherapy, Biotechnology, and other health sciences.
options.keyExams: NEET (very competitive — over 20 lakh students appear each year; requires serious, consistent preparation)
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3–5.5 years for a degree depending on the field

options.name: Paramedical Diploma after 10th (DMLT, ANM, X-Ray Tech, etc.)
options.whatIsIt: Short diploma courses (1–2 years) in medical lab technology, nursing assistance, radiography, and similar fields. You can start working in hospitals relatively quickly.
options.whoFor: Students who want to enter the healthcare field sooner without the long NEET route.
options.leadsTo: Lab technician, X-ray technician, nursing aide, hospital support roles. You can pursue further degrees later.
options.keyExams: Most programs are direct admission or state-level entrance; check your state's paramedical council for details

options.name: 12th Commerce
options.whatIsIt: You study Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, and optionally Maths. Leads to business and finance careers.
options.whoFor: Students interested in business, finance, or entrepreneurship — less relevant if your strength is in Science and Maths.
options.leadsTo: CA (Chartered Accountant), B.Com, BBA, MBA, banking, finance, and business roles.
options.keyExams: CA Foundation (after 12th), CUET, DU entrance, IPM (IIM Indore) for BBA
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3–5 years for a professional degree

options.name: 12th Arts / Humanities
options.whatIsIt: You study subjects like History, Geography, Political Science, Psychology, Sociology, and languages. A broad and flexible stream.
options.whoFor: Students interested in social sciences, law, journalism, civil services, or teaching — less aligned with a science-maths interest.
options.leadsTo: BA, B.A. LLB, Journalism, Social Work, UPSC Civil Services, teaching, and many other fields.
options.keyExams: CLAT (law), CUET, state university entrance exams
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3–5 years for a degree

sections.title: Job & earning options
sections.intro: Since you want to both study and earn, these are practical ways to build skills and income alongside your studies. Start small — even one skill developed now can fund your future education.

options.name: Freelance Graphic Design (Canva / Figma)
options.whatIsIt: Learn to create social media posts, logos, and banners using free tools like Canva. Figma is used for UI/UX design. Both can be learned for free on YouTube.
options.whoFor: Students who are creative and comfortable with computers or smartphones.
options.leadsTo: Freelance income on Fiverr/Internshala, and eventually a full career in UI/UX design or digital marketing.
options.timeCommitment: 1–3 months to learn basics; freelancing can start part-time from home

options.name: Content Creation (YouTube / Instagram Reels)
options.whatIsIt: Create short educational or entertaining videos related to Maths, science tricks, or any topic you enjoy. Consistency matters more than expensive equipment.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy explaining things, are comfortable on camera, and can commit to posting regularly.
options.leadsTo: Ad revenue, brand sponsorships, and a personal brand that can support future career growth.
options.timeCommitment: Ongoing — results typically appear after 6–12 months of consistent effort

options.name: Data Entry / Typing Jobs
options.whatIsIt: Basic computer work like filling forms, entering data, or transcribing text. Requires a computer and decent typing speed (30+ words per minute).
options.whoFor: Students who have access to a computer and want simple, low-skill entry-level online income.
options.leadsTo: Immediate part-time income; can upgrade to virtual assistant or data analysis roles over time.
options.timeCommitment: A few hours per day; typing speed can be improved in 2–4 weeks

options.name: Mobile Phone Repair
options.whatIsIt: Learn to repair cracked screens, replace batteries, and fix software issues on smartphones. Skills can be learned through YouTube and short local courses.
options.whoFor: Students who are technically curious, good with their hands, and interested in electronics — a great fit for a science-maths student.
options.leadsTo: Part-time income from neighbours and local shops; can grow into a full repair business or lead into electronics engineering interest.
options.timeCommitment: 1–2 months to learn basics; can start earning part-time after that

options.name: Tally / Basic Accounting
options.whatIsIt: Tally is the most widely used accounting software in Indian businesses. Even a basic Tally certificate makes you eligible for part-time bookkeeping work.
options.whoFor: Students who are comfortable with numbers and want a practical, in-demand skill.
options.leadsTo: Part-time data entry and accounts assistant work; useful if you later enter Commerce or B.Com.
options.timeCommitment: 1–2 months for a basic Tally course (available at local computer institutes)

options.name: Photography / Videography
options.whatIsIt: Offer local photography services for events, products, or reels using even a good smartphone. Editing skills (CapCut, Lightroom) add extra value.
options.whoFor: Students with a creative eye and a decent smartphone.
options.leadsTo: Freelance income from weddings, local businesses, and events; can grow into a full creative career.
options.timeCommitment: 2–4 weeks to learn basic editing; can start taking paid work within a month

options.name: Meesho / Product Reselling
options.whatIsIt: Resell products from Meesho or similar apps to your network via WhatsApp and Instagram without holding any inventory. Zero investment to start.
options.whoFor: Students who have an active social network and basic smartphone access.
options.leadsTo: Small but real income; builds an understanding of sales, marketing, and business that is useful long-term.
options.timeCommitment: A few hours per week; income depends on how actively you promote

options.name: Tailoring / Stitching
options.whatIsIt: Learn stitching and garment making through a short local course. Demand is consistent in every neighbourhood.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy hands-on craft work and want a skill that can generate income from home.
options.leadsTo: Home-based income from alterations and custom stitching; can grow into a small boutique business.
options.timeCommitment: 2–6 months for a basic tailoring course

guide.closingMessage: Save this guide and discuss it with your parents or teachers. When you finish 12th, come back to SkillKite — we'll build you a detailed career roadmap. Share it with a friend who needs it too. 🪁
